_note: Industry feed: Space / EO / climate / maritime news in dry insider voice. Refreshed every ~3 weeks by GHA cron.

items.headline: Blue Origin's New Glenn explodes on pad at Cape Canaveral during static fire test
items.snark: Cleared by the FAA on Friday, blown up by Thursday night, and Blue Moon Mk.2 is still on the manifest for Artemis 3 next year.

items.headline: Polish SAR startup Eycore launches first satellite, plans $14M manufacturing expansion
items.snark: Second private SAR operator in Europe, already talking to unnamed global integrators for sensors, which means the pitch deck is making the rounds.

items.headline: EarthDaily Analytics deploys six satellites, signs NRO contract and eight-figure defense AI deal
items.snark: They said it themselves: this is not a routine launch update, which is exactly what you say when government contracts finally justify the constellation spend.

items.headline: Rocket Lab awarded $90M Space Force contract for two GEO satellites with Heimdall space domain awareness payloads
items.snark: First GEO production program for Rocket Lab extends their vertical integration from Electron to the belt, and the GEOST acquisition is already paying off.

items.headline: Safran acquires Kayrros geospatial intelligence unit after energy assets sold to Energy Aspects
items.snark: Kayrros gets carved up: defense to Safran, energy analytics to Energy Aspects, and the one geospatial AI capability they actually built now serves two different markets.

items.headline: Global Fishing Watch maps 100% of industrial fishing fleet, triples detection capacity with Sentinel-2 integration
items.snark: Optical imagery closed the coastal gap that AIS and SAR left open, and now 291 million square kilometers of ocean are under satellite watch whether you broadcast or not.

items.headline: Environmental Justice Foundation report documents labor abuses and illegal fishing across 60 Chinese squid vessels in Southeast Pacific
items.snark: Eighty-one crew interviews, released days before the SPRFMO annual meeting, and still no comment from the delegation or the operators.

items.headline: North Pacific Fisheries Commission adopts IUU fishing measures but fails to protect Emperor Seamount Chain from bottom trawling
items.snark: NPFC advances electronic monitoring systems and shared data, then sets Pacific saury catch limits above the 2024 harvest rule they already agreed to.

items.headline: WMO predicts 91% chance global temperatures exceed 1.5C above pre-industrial for at least one year between 2026 and 2030
items.snark: The 1.5C threshold was a diplomatic line in the sand, now it's a near-certain annual exceedance within the forecast window.

items.headline: UK sets new May heat record at 35.1C in London as heat dome traps North African air over Western Europe
items.snark: Thirty-degree peaks in May used to be rare enough to remember individually, and now London is adding air conditioning installs faster than the grid can plan for them.

items.headline: Nature Climate Change: Microplastics and nanoplastics in atmosphere can exceed black carbon's regional radiative forcing
items.snark: Airborne plastic particles now interact with sunlight enough to influence the climate system, which means the radiative forcing budget just got another line item.

items.headline: Amazon acquires Globalstar for $11.57B to accelerate Amazon Leo deployment ahead of FCC mid-year deadline
items.snark: Regulatory arbitrage in action: Globalstar's MSS licenses have softer deployment requirements, so Amazon gets a fallback if the 1,600-satellite milestone slips.
